chunk_summary: This section discusses monitoring linear growth in children with stunting, focusing on short-interval indicators, growth velocity assessment, and escalation in non-responders based on adherence and clinical red flags.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of monitoring short-interval process indicators in stunting management.
Learn how to assess growth velocity and interpret deviations from age and pubertal norms.
Identify criteria for defining non-response and when to escalate evaluation or refer cases.
key_facts: Short-interval process indicators include meal structure, protein intake, supplements, and morbidity reduction (tracked over 2–4 weeks).
Height trajectory changes require 8–12 weeks; midchildhood growth velocity reference is 5–6 cm/year.
Non-response is defined as no improvement in growth velocity or continued downward height Z score after 8–12 weeks of adherence.
Red flags for escalation include height < -3 SD, pubertal arrest, chronic diarrhea, organomegaly, or persistent systemic symptoms.
Adolescents' growth velocity must be interpreted with pubertal stage due to delayed puberty mimicking growth failure.
reference_quote: World Health Organization. Growth reference data for 5–19 years: Indicators — https://www.who.int/tools/growth-reference-data-for-5to19-years/indicato...
document_key_topics: Monitoring linear growth in stunted children
Defining and managing non-response in growth interventions
Escalation criteria based on clinical red flags
